Field Label:
Please circle the number between 1 and 7 which you feel best fits the following statements. This refers to your usual way of life within the last week.
1 indicates "strongly disagree" and 7 indicates "strongly agree."
1. My motivation is lower when I am fatigued.
4. Fatigue interferes with my physical functioning.
5. Fatigue causes frequent problems for me.
6. My fatigue prevents sustained physical functioning.
7. Fatigue interferes with carrying out certain duties and responsibilities.
8. Fatigue is among my most disabling symptoms.
9. Fatigue interferes with my work, family, or social life.
Please mark an "X" on the number line which describes your global fatigue with 0 being worst and 10 being normal.

Section Header: Subjective Index of Physical and Social Outcome (SIPSO)
Field Label: Please answer all questions based on your experience since your stroke.
1. Since your stroke, how much difficulty do you have dressing yourself fully?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 4, No difficulty at all | 3, Slight difficulty | 2, Some difficulty | 1, A lot of difficulty | 0, I cannot dress myself fully

Field Label: 2. Since your stroke, how much difficulty do you have moving around all areas of the home?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 4, No difficulty at all | 3, Slight difficulty | 2, Some difficulty | 1, A lot of difficulty | 0, I cannot move around all areas of the home

Field Label: 3. Since your stroke, how satisfied are you with your overall ability to perform daily activities in and around the home?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 4, Completely satisfied | 3, Mostly satisfied | 2, Fairly satisfied | 1, Not very satisfied | 0, Completely dissatisfied

Field Label: 4. Since your stroke, how much difficulty do you have shopping for and carrying a few items (1 bag of shopping or less) when at the shops?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 4, No difficulty at all | 3, Slight difficulty | 2, Some difficulty | 1, A lot of difficulty | 0, I cannot shop for and carry a few items

Field Label: 5. Since your stroke, how independent are you in your ability to move around your local neighbourhood?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 4, I am completely independent | 3, I prefer to have someone else with me | 2, I need occasional assistance from someone | 1, I need assistance much of the time | 0, I am completely dependent on others

Field Label: 6. Since your stroke, how often do you feel bored with your free time at home?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 4, I am never bored with my free time | 3, A little of my free time | 2, Some of my free time | 1, Most of my free time | 0, All of my free time

Field Label: 7. Since your stroke, how would you describe the amount of communication between you and your friends/associates?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 4, A great deal | 3, Quite a lot | 2, Some | 1, A little bit | 0, None

Field Label: 8. Since your stroke, how satisfied are you with the level of interests and activities you share with your friends/associates?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 4, Completely satisfied | 3, Mostly satisfied | 2, Fairly satisfied | 1, Not very satisfied | 0, Completely dissatisfied

Field Label: 9. Since your stroke, how often do you visit friends/others?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 4, Most days | 3, At least once a week | 2, At least once a fortnight | 1, Once a month or less | 0, Never

Field Label: 10. Since your stroke, how do you feel about your appearance when out in public?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 4, Perfectly happy | 3, Slightly self-conscious | 2, Fairly self-conscious | 1, Very self-conscious | 0, I try to avoid going out in public

Field Label: Flexors: biceps and finger flexors (at least one)

Flexor synergy: Hand from contralateral knee to ipsilateral ear. From extensor synergy (shoulder adduction/ internal rotation, elbow extension, forearm pronation) to flexor synergy (shoulder abduction/ external rotation, elbow flexion, forearm supination).
Extensor synergy: Hand from ipsilateral ear to the contralateral knee.
